items.title: Anticipatory Driving Maneuver for Safety and Comfort: Temporal Graph Cross Attention-Based Front Vehicle Trajectory Prediction
items.venue: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
items.authors: Hyunsik Min, Gyeongseon Baek, Yeeun Kim, Byeongjoon Noh
items.summary: This paper presents a temporal graph cross-attention framework for front-vehicle trajectory prediction with an emphasis on anticipatory driving, passenger comfort, and safety support.
items.topic_copy: Temporal graph cross attention for safer and more comfortable driving support.
items.image_alt: Temporal graph cross attention trajectory prediction model diagram

items.title: SolarNexus: A Deep Learning Framework for Adaptive Photovoltaic Power Generation Forecasting and Scalable Management
items.summary: This paper introduces an adaptive photovoltaic forecasting framework that connects plant-level prediction accuracy with scalable energy-management workflows.
items.topic_copy: Adaptive forecasting and scalable management for photovoltaic systems.

items.title: TRACS-LLM: LLM-based Traffic Accident Criminal Sentencing Prediction Focusing on Imprisonment, Probation, and Fines
items.summary: This paper studies traffic-accident sentencing prediction by combining legal text, structured case attributes, and language-model reasoning for imprisonment, probation, and fine outcomes.
items.topic_copy: Language-model reasoning for traffic-accident sentencing outcomes.
items.search_terms: TRACS-LLM: LLM-based Traffic Accident Criminal Sentencing Prediction Focusing on Imprisonment, Probation, and Fines Artificial Intelligence and Law Hyunsik Min, Byeongjoon Noh This paper studies traffic-accident sentencing prediction by combining legal text, structured case attributes, and language-model reasoning for imprisonment, probation, and fine outcomes. International Journals legal ai traffic accident sentencing llm artificial intelligence and law

items.title: SolarFlux Predictor: A Novel Deep Learning Approach for Photovoltaic Power Forecasting in South Korea
items.authors: Hyunsik Min, Seokjun Hong, Jeonghoon Song, Byeoungmin Son, Byeongjoon Noh, Jihoon Moon
items.summary: This paper proposes a deep-learning approach for photovoltaic power forecasting in South Korea with an emphasis on stable national-scale prediction performance.
items.topic_copy: Deep-learning forecasting for photovoltaic power generation.
